one_line_ask: Batch color-grade six existing Summit & Sable product photos to one consistent house look, then non-destructively recolor the recovery-strap hardware and label accents on two hero shots — keeping lighting, shadow and texture natural — and export web-ready catalog images plus a review board.
brief: Summit & Sable is a direct-to-consumer overland and off-road recovery gear brand refreshing its online catalog for the new season. We are NOT reshooting — we need our existing product photos cleaned up and made consistent, plus a couple of color variants generated from shots we already have, exactly the way our old Photoshop editor used to do it. We're handing off six existing product photos (JPEG, roughly 2400x3000, shot in mixed lighting across two sessions so they don't match): a red recovery strap coiled on white sweep, the same strap's anodized aluminum snatch-hook shackle in close-up, a soft shackle, a folding shovel, a recovery tracks pair, and a branded ratchet strap with our printed label visible. Two jobs. First, batch grade: run all six through one consistent HOUSE LOOK so the catalog reads as a matched set — auto-tone the flat ones, correct the cool/warm white-balance mismatch between the two sessions to a neutral daylight, even out exposure, add a touch of vibrance so the products pop on a white PDP background, and lock a single Lightroom-style preset across the batch so contrast and tone are identical. Do not blow out the whites or crush the blacks; catalog images mus
